{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}}

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Worship Hall |location = Kyoto City, Kamigyo Ward, within Kyoto Gyoen 9 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|01|08.4|N|135|45|40.6|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Establishment = Traditionally Enryaku 14 (795 CE) |Main Hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with Hinoki bark roofing |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = September 15 |Rituals = }} [[File:Munakata-jinja (Kyoto Gyoen), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located within the Kyoto Gyoen in Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto City. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == Munakata Shrine enshrines the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Takiiribime|ja|多紀理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, as its main deities. Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. The shrine is believed to have been dedicated to the Munakata Goddesses under the order of Emperor Kanmu to protect the imperial court. {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was later merged into the shrine and believed to have been revered for its connection to the imperial family and other historical events. == History == === Establishment === According to tradition, Munakata Shrine was established in the 14th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] (795 CE) under the orders of Emperor Kanmu. [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]] enshrined the goddesses on the southwestern corner of his residence, known as the "Tokyo Residence" (東京第), as a protective measure for the imperial court. Historical records, including the ''土右記'' by {{ill|Minamoto no Toshifusa|ja|源師房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Toshifusa|1=Minamoto no Morofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564069}}, suggest that the site was chosen based on divine revelations received by Fuyutsugu while he served the imperial household. === Later Developments === The shrine gained prominence as the "birthplace" of Emperor Seiwa in 850 CE, which led to the Munakata Goddesses being granted higher Ranks, eventually reaching {{ill|Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin’i|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階|1=shinkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior First Rank in 864 CE. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari. According to lore, the shrine was established after a fox appeared in a dream of Fujiwara no Mototsune. * Shōshōi Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}. It serves as a temporary resting place for deities during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. * Konpira Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} and Emperor [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Sutoku]]. * Hanayama Inari Shrine: A shrine under a 600-year-old camphor tree within the grounds. == Access == The shrine is located within Kyoto Gyoen and can be reached via public transportation from Kyoto City. It is a 10-minute walk from the nearest bus stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Keiwa Izumoji, ''Historical and Cultural Sites of Kamigyo,'' Kyoto North Rotary Club, 1978. * Miyachi Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi, ''Shinto Encyclopedia,'' Rin-sensha, 1969 (Revised Edition). * ''The Topography of Kyoto,'' Heibonsha, 1979. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} * {{Wayback|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1701|title=Takiiribime Shrine|date=20041121152814}} * [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category_id=7&tourism_id=666 Munakata Shrine - Kyoto Travel Navi] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|version=94851838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{Commons category|Munakata-jinja (Kyoto)}} [[zh:宗像神社 (京都市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11451877}} [[Category:Buildings in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Heian-kyō]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kazan'in family]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Additionally, the shrine includes {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja|天岩戸開神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Iwato-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as auxiliary deities. Over time, the shrine came under the patronage of the {{ill|Kajuji family|ja|花山院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajuji family|1=Kazan'in family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615289}}, who incorporated additional deities into the site. Despite suffering damage during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the shrine was rebuilt and continued to be a significant site of worship. It was designated as a {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] and remains a place of reverence within Kyoto Gyoen. === Divine Rankings === * [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE): Elevated to Senior Second Rank. * Jōgan 6 (864 CE): Elevated to Junior First Rank. * [[:en:Kenji (era)|Kenchō]] 1 (1275 CE): Included in imperial offerings for major festivals. == Festivals and Rituals == The shrine's main annual festival is held on September 15. It includes traditional rituals honoring the shrine's deities for their protection and blessings. == Shrine Architecture == The main hall is constructed in the one-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style with Hinoki bark roofing. The current structure dates to the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], having been rebuilt after previous destruction. == Sub-Shrines == * Prosperity Inari Shrine: Dedicated to Myōbu Inari.
